title: Foreign or local? Investors at lossover GIFT funds
summary: Investors are grappling with confusion when it comes to reporting GIFT City fund investments in their tax returns. Fund houses provide varying guidance on whether these should be categorized as foreign assets. While GIFT City falls under the foreign category per FEMA regulations, it's treated as a domestic entity for income tax.

title: Tata, SP Group explore share swap to unlock Tata Sons stake amid valuation divide

Their recent refinancing attempts indicate a solid confidence level in the pledged Tata Sons shares.

title: HSBC offers 19x FCNR(B) leverage in GIFT City
summary: HSBC's GIFT City unit offers NRIs significant leverage on FCNR deposits.  An NRI can borrow up to nineteen times their own funds for deposits.  This special deposit program attracts overseas dollar deposits and boosts inflows.  The Reserve Bank of India temporarily relaxed interest rate ceilings for this purpose.  This initiative aims to attract foreign exchange until September 2026.

title: MSCI rejig could bring $2.3 billion into Indian stocks
summary: An upcoming MSCI index rebalancing may introduce about $2.3 billion in passive investment flows.  The global index provider will announce changes on August 12 after market close.  Several Indian stocks are likely candidates for inclusion in the MSCI India Standard Index.  These changes will impact index-linked exchange-traded funds and mutual funds.  The rebalancing will become effective for portfolios on August 31.

title: Private insurers seek tweaks in unlisted investment plan
summary: Private insurers want Irdai to change proposed investment norms for unlisted companies. They suggest linking exposure limits to shareholders' funds instead of surplus. This change could unlock billions for investment in privately held businesses. The proposed framework may expand sector investment capacity significantly. Insurers seek greater flexibility in managing investment portfolios.

title: RBI bars banks, NBFCs from selling acquired stressed assets back to defaulting borrowers, related parties
summary: New Reserve Bank of India norms will prevent lenders from reselling acquired assets to defaulting borrowers. These prudential rules apply to banks, small finance banks, and NBFCs starting October 2026. Specified non-financial assets are those obtained when resolving stressed loans from borrowers. Lenders must establish board-approved policies for acquiring and disposing of these assets. These assets will be disclosed separately on balance sheets, not as NPAs.
